prompt: My windowed Kafka processor has been running for an hour but produced no output. State is STARTED and connections are healthy

assertions.name: identifies_idle_partition_issue
assertions.description: Response identifies idle Kafka partitions as the likely root cause
assertions.reason: This is the most common cause of windowed processors producing no output when healthy

assertions.name: recommends_partitionIdleTimeout
assertions.description: Response recommends adding partitionIdleTimeout to Kafka $source

assertions.name: provides_specific_syntax
assertions.description: Response provides specific JSON syntax for partitionIdleTimeout configuration

assertions.name: explains_window_closure_dependency
assertions.description: Response explains that windows won't close if a partition has no data

assertions.name: provides_fix_steps
assertions.description: Response provides ordered steps: stop processor → modify pipeline → restart

assertions.name: does_not_assume_connection_problem
assertions.description: Does NOT suggest connection issues (prompt states connections are healthy)

assertions.name: does_not_assume_source_has_no_data
assertions.description: Does NOT suggest source has no data (running for an hour implies data exists)

root_cause: Idle Kafka partitions blocking window closure
explanation: Windowed processors wait for data from ALL partitions before closing a window. If any partition goes idle (no new data), the window never closes and no output is produced.

solution.explanation: After 30 seconds of no data from a partition, treat it as idle and allow window to close

fix_steps: Modify pipeline to add partitionIdleTimeout to $source
Restart processor: atlas-streams-manage action='start-processor' with resumeFromCheckpoint=false to reprocess
diagnostic_pattern: State: STARTED + zero output + windowed pipeline → likely idle Kafka partitions blocking window closure
